key_points: Freshman housing generally adequate but hills make mobility challenging
Off-campus housing market has bad landlords with little accountability
Students built a landlord review site due to 'awful housing situations'
key_quotes: If you twist your ankle/break a leg, you're going to have a really hard time getting around campus
I remember I had the one landlord who inspired Bethlehem to enact a law saying landlords are required to live within 15 miles
Question: You mentioned you lived off campus as a freshman, did they not have enough housing for all of the freshman class?

key_points: Greek life dominates — non-fraternity guys struggle to access parties
Work hard, play hard culture is real and widely confirmed
Alternative/non-preppy students (metalheads, minorities) wonder if they'll fit in
key_quotes: If ur a guy not in Greek life you lowkey have to get a fake to get to parties or know the right people
If you're a girl you determine your social life. It's harder for guys
I have toured and always felt that it was a super preppy school — are there any Metalheads/alternative communities?

key_points: Rathbone dining hall is beloved, especially late-night study snacks
Meal plans are overpriced and structured so students regularly lose money
Food quality is decent but plan flexibility is the real problem
key_quotes: Meal plans are awful now, food is pretty good but plans are overpriced and you'll often lose money because you didn't spend your whole plan allocation
My favorite thing: make a waffle, put a scoop of ice cream on top, eat it while doing work with a view
Rathbone's midnight study snacks during finals helped me with calc

key_points: Freshman adjustment is genuinely hard, especially for shy students
Steep hills and physical campus create real stress for mobility-limited students
Academic pressure is high enough that some students question their major choice entirely
key_quotes: My daughter is a freshman and is having a really hard time. I know it's only been a few days but any advice on how a shy person can make friends?
I am a first year here and I have hated the thought of me in the business school. I feel a lack of interest… I am miserable
If you have to take out loans for full tuition, you will pay it back until your mid 40s

key_points: Stingy with merit aid — many students get zero despite strong applications
Lehigh Commitment grant helps low-income students but middle-class gets squeezed
Appeals to financial aid office can work — students report success calling and 'begging'
key_quotes: Accepted ED1, got legitimately 9k in scholarship. For a school that costs 80k per year. Widow mom making 150k made them think we could pay full price
I heard that if you call the financial aid office and basically beg for money they will give you aid
55% of Lehigh students pay full price, so the other 45% that qualify for financial aid don't pay near full tuition

key_points: Engineering and business are strong; IBE program is a standout differentiator
Grade deflation is a real concern, especially in math and pre-med tracks
Professors are hit-or-miss but standout faculty are frequently mentioned by alumni
key_quotes: Don't think there's grade deflation there, but a 3.8+ as a premed probably isn't the easiest thing
Meng-Sang Chew was tough as heck and a lot of people failed out — but if you put in the effort, you actually learned
Tons of opportunities: research, field work, fellowships, internships, clubs. Almost everyone I know is involved in something cool

key_points: Financial aid office is reachable and sometimes responsive to appeals
Admissions fraud case exposed weak vetting of international scholarship credentials
Communication around decisions and billing is confusing for many students
key_quotes: Emailed my financial aid officer and they were actually really helpful
How dumb are the people who work in admission to provide a full scholarship without even checking the credentials?
The university did not lose a thing, so it was easy for them to forgive the debt — it was the donors who bore the weight

key_points: Campus is visually stunning — consistently praised by students and alumni
The hills are brutal and a genuine daily obstacle, especially in winter
Bethlehem is quiet; SteelStacks is a highlight but the city isn't exciting
key_quotes: The hills are ferocious
Tomorrow is cold as hell, lowkey just travel through main campus because Bethlehem isn't very scenic
Visit Linderman library and University Center — if you're on campus, these are must-sees

key_points: Strong job placement for accounting, finance, and engineering grads
Alumni network is tight and actively helpful — job connections through parties reported
Post-2023 market crash hurt outcomes; one grad warned of stepping down from your major
key_quotes: I got my current job from connecting with someone at a party after I graduated
Good job placement for Accounting, Finance, and Engineering — which are pretty employable degrees anyway
I graduated Lehigh in 2023 when the market crashed… you will definitely be getting a job within your major and actually maybe even a step down

key_points: Full price (~$90k/year) is widely considered unjustifiable, especially vs. Penn State
Multiple students chose state schools after Lehigh aid packages left $80k+ in debt
If you get generous aid or a merit scholarship, the value proposition flips entirely
key_quotes: Lehigh is probably better, Lehigh is definitely not 200k more in debt better
If you have to take out loans for full tuition, you will pay it back until your mid 40s. That's the biggest thing
It's basically Diet Ivy League — if you can afford it or have good grants, it's awesome

key_points: Preppy, affluent, Greek-dominated — but welcoming to those who engage
Strong sense of school pride and tight community among students who lean in
Minority and non-traditional students can thrive but need to seek out their communities
key_quotes: Lehigh definitely fits the bill of a typical PWI. Big frat culture, affluent student population. However, I have NEVER felt excluded as a POC
I'm openly trans and nobody's ever said anything offensive to me — Lehigh is extremely inclusive
Put yourself out there is the number one thing. Talk to everyone and don't assume someone's character by their appearance

key_points: **Financial Aid:** Most middle-class families pay near full price with minimal merit aid
**Antisemitism:** Recent swastika incidents reported on campus by current students
**Fraud/Oversight:** International student ran scholarship fraud undetected; university forgave debt without prosecution
key_quotes: Recent antisemitism with swastikas apparently
He resorted to cheating in university exams while spending most of his time drinking — also made money from fraudulent internships
The university has a duty to pursue justice against this criminal — instead, they are stepping away to avoid accountability for their own lack of diligence

key_points: Steam tunnel system is a legendary student secret worth exploring after graduation
Rathbone late-night food during finals is a genuine campus comfort institution
IBE (Integrated Business and Engineering) is a rare dual-degree program that employers notice
key_quotes: If anyone wants to get from the library to Hawks Nest employees lounge completely underground, this is your guide
Rathbone's midnight study snacks during finals helped me with calc — the number of hot dogs I could eat: the limit did not exist
The Asa Packer duck 3D prints were unironically a decently large part of why I chose to ED to Lehigh
